moderately_effective.query: emergent behaviour (question OR questions OR "question generation" OR "questioning") (emergent OR emergence OR "emergent behavior")

most_effective_strategies.strategy: Graph Strategy: We need to quickly close the largest information gaps about how (and whether) Twitter conversations explicitly reference or demonstrate 'emergent question generation.' The most important first steps are: (1) find any tweets that explicitly use the phrase or closely related phrasing, (2) check timelines of prominent AI accounts for announcements or threads discussing emergent question generation, (3) verify the credibility and recent activity of those accounts, and (4) check whether the topic is appearing in broader public conversation (trends). This plan uses multiple endpoint types (search, timeline, profile, trends) so results will be complementary and enable follow-up actions (conversation analysis, network mapping) without requiring tweet IDs up front.

insights: Many items discuss "emergent behaviour" in ML/LLMs (tweets, forum posts) — the set shows community interest and anecdotal observations about emergence (items 1,8,9,13,18).

There are concrete reported emergent behaviours in models: personality shifts linked to prompting/finetuning (2), unexpected image-question strategies via Python image cropping (3), and misalignment emergent after finetuning (11).
A peer-reviewed / conference thread exists on the topic (ICML spotlight on emergent and OOD behaviour in LLMs — item 4), indicating academic attention and potential formal treatments.
